mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce discount rates for equity valuations, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no positive correlation with falling FEDFUNDS over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 6.0 percent signals severe labor market deterioration, triggering recession fears that drive S&P 500 returns negative over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in over 65% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 6.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand expansion, generating positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day SPX returns with a Rank IC above 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects rapid capacity utilization and corporate earnings growth, pushing S&P 500 returns positive over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases yield negative 5-day forward returns in post-shock recovery periods, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation combined with low policy rates creates a real rate inversion that forces eventual aggressive tightening, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction of high CPIAUCSL and low FEDFUNDS predicts positive 20-day returns in over 60% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payrolls growth exceeding 200 thousand signals robust economic momentum and wage growth, supporting positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative in more than 55% of months where PAYEMS growth surpasses 200 thousand.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real GDP growth reflects sustained aggregate demand strength, which translates directly into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth shows a negative or statistically insignificant correlation with 60-day forward SPX returns over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production alongside rising unemployment indicates a synchronized supply and labor contraction, forcing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The joint signal of falling INDPRO and rising UNRATE fails to predict negative 5-day returns, with accuracy below 50% in triggered instances.
